raw/articles/AUC-curve.md.key_insight: Stub source — AUC-ROC is the area under the ROC curve, a threshold-independent classifier metric; see pr-auc for the imbalanced-data alternative

raw/articles/Pr-auc-and-pr-auc.md.key_insight: PR-AUC focuses on the positive class for imbalanced data; PR-AUC Lift normalizes it by class prevalence to enable cross-dataset comparison

raw/articles/finetuning_and_tuning.md.key_insight: Fine-tuning adapts pre-trained model weights to new tasks; hyperparameter tuning optimizes training configuration — they are orthogonal and often done together

raw/articles/python-protocol.md.key_insight: Python Protocol enables static duck typing — any class implementing the required methods satisfies the Protocol without inheritance, enabling flexible type-safe interfaces

raw/articles/stl-and-mstl0-decomposition.key_insight: STL and MSTL are mathematically identical for a single period when parameters match; MSTL adds automation and multi-seasonality support

raw/articles/rl.md.key_insight: RL algorithms are combinations of three primitives (value function, policy, model), with Actor-Critic at the intersection as the dominant modern framework

raw/articles/understanding_bellman_expec.key_insight: R(s) in the Bellman Expectation Equation is the expected immediate reward for a state; fixing a policy in an MDP collapses it into an MRP

raw/articles/reward-is-enough-hypothesis.key_insight: The Reward is Enough hypothesis argues all intelligence emerges from reward maximization; delayed consequences and the MDP/POMDP distinction are foundational RL challenges

raw/articles/rl-introduction.md.key_insight: Sutton & Barto Chapter 1 defines RL as a third ML paradigm built on four elements (policy, reward, value function, model); the exploration-exploitation tradeoff and TD learning are its distinguishing features
